Tests the behavior of `Number.prototype.toString` after deletion in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code snippet performs a test on the `Number.prototype.toString` method. It first deletes the `toString` method from `Number.prototype`, which removes the default string conversion behavior for number objects. After the deletion, the code uses an `assert` statement to check if calling `Number.prototype.toString()` returns the string `"[object Number]"`. This test ensures that after the deletion, the `toString` method falls back to the default `Object.prototype.toString` behavior, which returns the type of the object in a string format. The code is a simple script that demonstrates the behavior of JavaScript's prototype chain and method overriding.
